title: Wi-Fi Router — No Internet Connection
prerequisites: Have access to the router's power cable
Know the location of the router

steps.customer_message: Let's start with a simple restart. Please turn off your router by unplugging the power cable from the wall socket. Wait 30 seconds, then plug it back in. Once the lights are stable, let me know.
steps.agent_notes: Power cycle resolves most transient connectivity issues.

steps.customer_message: No problem! The power cable is the thick cable going into the back of your router. Unplug it from the wall (not from the router). Wait 30 seconds, then plug it back in. The router lights will blink, then settle. Once stable — usually 1-2 minutes — let me know!
Great! Now check on your phone or computer — can you see your Wi-Fi network name in the available networks list?
steps.agent_notes: Verify SSID is broadcasting before checking connectivity.
response_buttons: Yes, I can see my network

steps.customer_message: On your phone, go to Settings → Wi-Fi (or Wireless). You should see a list of available networks. Look for your home network name. Do you see it?
Let's check the lights on your router. Is there a light labelled 'Internet' or 'WAN'? What colour is it — green, orange, or is it off?
steps.agent_notes: WAN/Internet LED indicates physical link to ISP.

steps.customer_message: Please connect your device to your Wi-Fi network and try opening a website like google.com. Does the page load?

Let's try one more thing. On the back of your router, there should be a small 'Reset' button (usually requires a pin). Hold it for 10 seconds until the lights blink. **Note:** This will reset your Wi-Fi password to the factory default, printed on a sticker on the router. Shall we try this?
steps.agent_notes: Factory reset as last self-service step. Warn about password reset.
response_buttons: No, I don't want to reset
steps.safety_note: This will erase all custom Wi-Fi settings. Note your current Wi-Fi password before proceeding.

steps.customer_message: After the reset and router restart, check if the internet is working now. Try opening a website.

terminal_states.message: That's great news! Your internet connection is back. If you need help again, feel free to chat with us anytime.
I've logged everything we tried and am connecting you with a support specialist who can help further. They'll be in touch shortly.
It looks like there may be an issue with your internet service provider's connection to your router. I'm escalating this so our team can coordinate with your ISP. Hold on!

escalation_conditions: Internet LED is off or orange after restart
Reset did not resolve the issue
